stories.title: EU’s top court finds Hungary’s anti-LGBTQ+ law in breach of key values
stories.summary: ECJ says law passed in 2021 is discriminatory and ‘contrary to the identity of the union’, in early test for new PM The EU’s highest court has found Hungary’s anti-LGBTQ+ law to be discriminatory, stigmatising and in breach of basic democratic values, setting up an early test for the incoming government when it takes power next month. In a wide-ranging judgment, the European court of justice said the 2021 law that bans content about LGBTQ+ people from schools and primetime TV was at odds with a

stories.title: From Attila the Hun to Donald J. Trump: A brief history of popes battling world leaders
stories.summary: Donald Trump wasn’t the first, and he likely won’t be the last. The U.S. president’s simmering feud with Pope Leo XIV is just the latest in a millennia-spanning tradition of world leaders beefing with the head of the Catholic Church. War in Iran joins the decline of the Roman Empire and the French Revolution as historic flashpoints that have triggered dust-ups between pontiffs and some of civilization’s most iconic political figures. The latest clash has its origin in the

stories.title: EU top court strikes down Hungary’s anti-LGBTQ+ rules
stories.summary: Hungary violated EU law when it banned children from accessing LGBTQ+ content, the Court of Justice of the European Union ruled Tuesday, ordering Budapest to scrap the legislation. The landmark judgment marks a blow to outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s legacy as his longtime rival Péter Magyar prepares to take office next month. Hungary’s legislative crackdown on LGBTQ+ representation is in breach of a series of EU laws and “constitute[s] a particularly serious interference with

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: EU moves to unlock €90bn loan as Orbán exits
stories.summary: Macron says ‘reasonably optimistic’ loan with go through after election defeat of pro-Russian Hungarian PM. What we know on day 1,518 European Union countries will on Wednesday move to unlock a €90bn loan for Ukraine as the defeated Viktor Orbán exits power in Hungary. The now-caretaker Hungarian prime minister, friendly to Vladimir Putin, had been blocking the loan but will soon be replaced by Peter Magyar, who won a sweeping electoral mandate. Magyar has promised smoother relations with Brusse

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Purdue Pharma expected to forfeit $225m as sentence in criminal opioids case
stories.summary: Under settlement, Sackler family will pay state, local and Native American tribal governments, individual victims and others Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A judge is expected to sentence OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma to forfeit $225m to the US justice department on Tuesday, clearing the way for the company to finalize a settlement of thousands of lawsuits it faces over its role in the opioid crisis. The penalty was agreed to in a 2020 pact to resol

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.title: Tuesday briefing: What it might take for lasting peace between the US and Iran
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: Our diplomatic editor on whether permanent peace is possible – or whether there will be a new escalation in the conflict Good morning. The Gulf is stuck in limbo between war and peace. Despite a ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, both sides have ramped up threats once again. A lasting end to the violence feels possible, but so does a renewed round of fighting – and more death, destruction and economic pain. JD Vance, the US vice-president, is expected to fly to Pakist

stories.title: Scarborn (Kos) review – rumbustuous period epic stirs up trouble in 18th-century Poland
stories.summary: When a veteran of the American war of independence returns to his native country with popular rule on his mind dangerous passions ignite This Polish historical drama is an odd duck of a feature but there’s definitely a cinematic flair to proceedings. For a start, a lot of it unfolds over one evening in a candlelit setting, so you may have to screw up your eyes in order to make out what’s going on. My advice is to then surrender to its strange tonality and weird flat stretches, because the ending

stories.title: EU optimistic over approval of €90bn loan for Ukraine following Orbán defeat – Europe live
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas confirms she expects ‘some positive decisions tomorrow’ on the €90bn loan German foreign minister Joseph Wadephul also makes it very clear that he is relieved with the change of government in Hungary, calling it “a breath of fresh air” and a promise of hope for Ukraine. He urged Hungary to drop its “unusual blockade” for policies for Ukraine “as quickly as possible,” pointing to what he argued was a clear pro-European mandate from the electorate in Hungary (it

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: From soldier to developer: How German troops are test building their own drones
stories.summary: During the "Grand Quadriga 2026" exercise, the German army has been demonstrating the transformation of modern warfare, where drones, data and speed are increasingly decisive.

stories.title: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.title: Mi történt a politikatudomány szerint Magyarországon 2026. április 12-én?
stories.summary: Elsöprő győzelmet ért el a Tisza a Fidesszel szemben április 12-én, de mi történt valójában? Hogyan viszonyult a demokrácia és a Fidesz által kiépített 16 éves rezsim a hazai politikai rendszerhez? Mit mond a szakirodalom és legfőképpen: mi az a választási forradalom?

stories.title: Nem garázsokkal és parkolóházakkal kellene megoldani Budapest parkolási problémáit
stories.summary: A magánparkolók támogatása rengeteg közpénzbe kerül, minimális a hatása, és a közterületek humanizálását is hátráltatja.

stories.title: Mennyit számított a választáson a jelöltek személye?
stories.summary: Öt olyan választókerületet találtunk, ahol nem annak a pártnak a jelöltje nyert, amelyik listán több szavazatot kapott. Az ál-Magyar Péter és a Mi Hazánk jó eséllyel be tudott kavarni az egyéni eredményeknél, Hadházy nem.

stories.title: A 444 szerint megkezdődött a Fideszben az Orbán Viktor-mentő hadművelet
stories.summary: A lap a távozó miniszterelnök közvetlen környezetéből származó értesülései szerint többen Orbán Balázst tartják felelősnek az eredményért.

stories.title: Júniusig nem kell szolidaritási hozzájárulást fizetnie Budapestnek
stories.summary: Az Államkincstár mentesítette a fővárost, és Szolnok is haladékot kapott.

stories.title: GKI: Sokkal több benzint tudunk venni ma a fizetésünkből, mint 2010-ben
stories.summary: Nem is annyira az olaj ára, inkább a magyar szabályozás dobálta azt az elmúlt másfél évtizedben, hogy egy átlagbért kereső magyar mennyi üzemanyagot tud venni a fizetéséből.

stories.title: Ügyészségi hatáskörbe vonta a legfőbb ügyész a Magyar Nemzeti Bankot érintő nyomozást
stories.summary: A Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség nyomoz tovább. Az ügyészség részletesen levezette, mi ennek az oka, azt írták, a büntetőeljárási törvény a nyomozást két részre osztja: felderítési és vizsgálati szakaszra, ahol a választóvonalat a gyanúsítotti kihallgatás jelenti. Eső után köpönyeg, reagált Magyar Péter a döntésre.

stories.title: Áll a bál a Muzsika TV körül: egy volt műsorvezető szerint a jogdíj felét oda kellett adni, ha valaki képernyőn akart maradni
stories.summary: A csatorna volt főszerkesztője szerint a vádak légből kapottak, egyesek a sikertelenséget magyarázzák így.

stories.title: Elbukta a kormány a pert, uniós jogot sért a melegeket pedofilokkal összemosó törvény
stories.summary: Ha a magyar állam nem változtat a jogszabályon, újabb per jöhet – már a pénzbüntetés megállapításáról.

stories.title: SZELEKTOR26: tripla lemezen az új magyar zenei generáció
stories.summary: Felpörög a hazai underground: színre lép az új generáció, májusban érkezik a Telekom Electronic Beats SZELEKTOR26 tripla válogatáslemeze, addig pedig hétről hétre jönnek a nyertes előadók. Ezúttal paks2 van soron: ő a CLUB album egyik legerősebb trackjének alkotója.

stories.title: Magyar Péter már kedden délután fogadja az iskolaigazgatókat az irodájában
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője délután négy órakor találkozik az igazgatókkal. Kedden emellett tíz miniszterelnökkel és államfővel, és Kelemen Hunorral, az RMDSZ elnökével is egyeztet.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Robert Ficóval tárgyalt telefonon, a Beneš-dekrétumokra hivatkozó jogszabály hatályon kívül helyezését kérte tőle
stories.summary: Abban állapodtak meg, hogy az Európai Tanács következő ülésén személyesen folytatják a tárgyalásokat Brüsszelben.

stories.title: Magyar Színházi Társaság: Az indulat beszűkíti a tisztánlátást, inkább valódi párbeszédre van szükség
stories.summary: A társaság hétfői közgyűlésén arról beszéltek, a színház mostantól nem lehet hatalmi logikák eszköze.

stories.title: Orbán quietly sidesteps his last EU summit after years of envisioning major battles at each one
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán is leaving the body of EU heads of state and government without a proper farewell on account of his duties related to the transfer of power, which will conveniently allow him to avoid answering questions about the Szijjártó-Lavrov phone calls.

stories.title: A fővárosban bevált fegyelmezett vezetőként, most egy tizennégyszer nagyobb feladatot kap
stories.summary: Bujdosó Andreát a Tisza fővárosi frakcióvezetőjeként szigor jellemezte. A Fidesz trükkjeire azzal válaszolt, hogy a kormánypárt nem a városban élők és a magyar emberek problémáival foglalkozik, ha pedig személyeskedő sértegetés kezdődött, akkor ő is beleállt a dologba. Most egy 141 fős frakciót kell egyben tartania, ami nagyon különböző hátterű emberekből áll.

stories.title: Robert Fico: A Beneš-dekrétumok kérdésében alapvetően eltérő álláspontot képviselünk
stories.summary: A szlovák miniszterelnök is posztolt a Magyar Péterrel folytatott telefonbeszélgetésről.

stories.title: Péter Magyar asked Robert Fico to revoke legislation referencing Beneš Decrees
stories.summary: The two politicians spoke on the phone and agreed to continue their discussion in person in Brussels during the upcoming meeting of the European Council.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Garanciát kaptam arra, hogy az RMDSZ a jövőben tartózkodni fog a magyar pártpolitikai küzdelembe való beavatkozástól
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője Kelemen Hunorral, az RMDSZ elnökével személyesen egyeztetett kedden. Beszéltek a levélszavazásról, és a későbbi szavazások szabályainak felülvizsgálatáról is.

stories.title: Pogány Induló már nem akar senkinek megfelelni
stories.summary: Felemás kísérlet vagy félkész mestermű? Pogány Induló Valamit majd kitalálok című harmadik lemeze megosztó lett, de ez egyben karrierjének eddigi legsokszínűbb vállalkozása.

stories.title: Megvan az Apple új vezetője, Tim Cook utódja
stories.summary: A hardverért felelős John Ternus veszi át Tim Cook vezérigazgatói posztját ősztől. Cook az igazgatótanács elnöke lesz.

stories.title: Kapitány István meglátogatta Nagy Mártont és Szijjártó Pétert
stories.summary: Kapitány Orbán Anitával és Kármán Andrással közösen tárgyalt az átadás-átvételi folyamatok elindításáról.

stories.title: Ókovács Szilveszter Magyar Péternek: Mi van, ha nem olvasok Telexet?
stories.summary: Az Operaház igazgatója szerint már adtak egy ajánlatot a kormánynak az október 23-i megemlékezés megtartására.

stories.summary: Annyira fontos a magyarok számára a krumpli, hogy elképzelni is nehéz, mennyire tiltakozott a fogyasztása ellen a nép még kétszáz évvel ezelőtt is.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: UK officials saw ‘no need’ to vet Peter Mandelson, sacked Olly Robbins claims
stories.summary: LONDON — Parts of Keir Starmer’s government believed there was “no need” to obtain full security vetting for Peter Mandelson before making him Britain’s ambassador to the U.S. in late 2024, a former top civil servant alleged Tuesday. Olly Robbins, who the prime minister sacked in a bitter row over the vetting process last week, said there was a “generally dismissive attitude” about the need to obtain clearance for the controversial Labour figure as time ticked down to Donald Trump’s inauguration

stories.title: US ‘restricts intelligence sharing with South Korea’ after minister identified suspected nuclear site
stories.summary: Washington reportedly limits satellite data after minister spoke publicly about suspected facility in North Korea The US has partly restricted intelligence sharing with South Korea after the country’s unification minister publicly identified a suspected North Korean nuclear site, according to reports in South Korean media. Chung Dong-young told lawmakers in March that North Korea was operating uranium enrichment facilities in Kusong, a north-western area that had not previously been officially c

stories.title: Requests for US legal aid linked to Palestine activism far surpass pre-2023 levels
stories.summary: Civil rights group logs 300% yearly rise from before war and says ‘authoritarian repression … went into overdrive’ A civil rights group dedicated to the defense of pro-Palestinian speech said that requests for legal assistance linked to Palestine-related activism in the US continues to far surpass pre-2023 levels, having logged 300% more requests for support last year than in any year prior to Israel’s war in Gaza. Palestine Legal logged some 1,131 requests in 2025. That was less than the record

stories.title: Japan loosens long-standing curbs on arms exports
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

stories.title: Russia faked economic data to appear more resilient to its war and sanctions, intel report says
stories.summary: Swedish intelligence said Russia has been covering up a weak, debt-driven economy hit by war costs and sanctions, warning of rising inflation and banking risks.

stories.title: France freezes €6B spending this year because of Middle East crisis
stories.summary: PARIS — France is putting €6 billion of spending on hold this year because of the economic fallout of the war in the Middle East. “In the current phase we cannot turn a blind eye to the war nor the debt,” Budget Minister David Amiel told reporters after a meeting with key ministers and lawmakers. “Faced with the €6 billion cost of the crisis, we anticipate €6 billion of spending that could be stopped.” Amiel said that extra tax revenue coming from higher fuel prices had already dropped by €100 m

stories.title: Woolworths engaged in ‘marketing magic’ to trick customers, consumer watchdog tells court
stories.summary: The trial between the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission and the supermarket giant began in the federal court in Sydney on Tuesday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Woolworths engaged in “marketing magic” to trick customers into thinking they were getting genuine discounts as part of the supermarket’s “Prices Dropped” promotion, the consumer regulator has told a court. stories.title: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuse The actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine. The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast

stories.title: Trump’s latest border wall plans to slice through Big Bend national park: ‘Our lives are being upended’
stories.summary: The rural Texas region, long spared, is being fast-tracked for the border wall amid bipartisan opposition Tractors suddenly appeared at the entrance to Chispa Road near the US-Mexico border in rural Big Bend, Texas, in late March. Contractors informed Yolanda Alvarado, a cattle rancher, that they were starting work to upgrade the rough county dirt road there into a “highway” – the first step needed for semi trucks to haul the 30-foot steel pillars used to build Donald Trump’s border barrier . “T

stories.title: Seven more arrested after arson attacks on London Jewish sites
stories.summary: Seven held over alleged plot to commit another attack and man held over drone incident near Israeli embassy Seven more people have been arrested after a series of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London. The Metropolitan police said the suspects were arrested in the past 48 hours over an alleged conspiracy to commit a further arson attack, although the specific target is not yet known. stories.title: Iran war: Talks in doubt as leaders issue threats
stories.summary: While the talks remain in limbo, the United States and Iran have been trading threats. Separately, media reports suggest US Vice President JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for ceasefire negotiations. DW has more.

stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.

stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.title: Why early action matters for cardiovascular health
stories.summary: Europe has an opportunity to address avoidable cardiovascular disease (CVD) risk and ease the burden of chronic and infectious diseases on national health systems. However, this requires a shift in EU policy from late-stage treatment to earlier detection and timely prevention of CVD. stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. stories.title: Israeli soldiers using sexual assault to force Palestinians out of West Bank, report says

stories.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
stories.summary: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. stories.title: FAA grounds Blue Origin's New Glenn after orbital setback

stories.title: Why the cloud still runs on coal and gas

stories.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker

stories.title: Taiwan blames pressure from China for nixed Africa trip
stories.summary: The Taiwanese government says Beijing used economic coercion to convince three African nations to revoke permission for President Lai Ching-te to traverse their airspace.

stories.title: Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic around Gulf, Straight of Hormuz
stories.summary: The passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz - the world's busiest oil shipping canal - has been severely disrupted amid the US-Israeli war with Iran. Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic in the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz from April 18 to April 20, amid a fragile US-Iran truce.

stories.title: No Iran delegation sent to talks with US yet as ceasefire set to expire

stories.title: Iran hangs man accused of ties to Israel and setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests

stories.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids

stories.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

stories.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume

stories.title: Carney says Canada’s strong economic ties to US are ‘weakness’ to be corrected
stories.summary: Prime minister details efforts to attract investment and sign trade deals with other countries in 10-minute video address Canada ’s strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, the country’s prime minister has warned. In a 10-minute video address, Mark Carney spoke about his government’s efforts to strengthen the Canadian economy by attracting new investments and signing trade deals with other countries. stories.title: Climate change impacts India's harvest festivals
stories.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

stories.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
stories.summary: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

stories.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
stories.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

stories.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
stories.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

stories.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
stories.summary: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

stories.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
stories.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

stories.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big

stories.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny

stories.title: Sudan war fuels child hunger crisis

stories.title: Two police officers suspended after fleeing Kyiv shooting scene
stories.summary: An inquiry was opened after a video showed officers fleeing in panic from Saturday’s shooting, which left six dead and many injured. The patrol police chief has resigned.

stories.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads

stories.title: Iran pulls out of Islamabad peace talks after US seizes cargo ship

stories.title: Carney says Canada must cut reliance on US as tariffs hit economy
stories.summary: The Canadian leader stressed that his country cannot hinge its future on hope that the US will return to the partner it formerly was to his country and that active steps must be taken to protect Ottawa from the disruptions caused by the Trump administration.

stories.title: Oil prices climb as US-Iran stand-off keeps Strait of Hormuz in limbo
stories.summary: Oil prices climbed more than 5% while European markets fell on Monday morning as a stand-off between Iran and the US prevented tankers from using the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Major fire in Malaysia destroys hundreds of homes, displaces thousands

stories.title: Louisiana gunman kills eight children in suspected domestic mass shooting

stories.title: Obama and Mamdani sing with kids during first meeting in New York

stories.title: Moment US navy fires on and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship

stories.title: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, Donald Trump has said.

stories.title: Community holds vigil after Louisiana mass shooting
stories.summary: Residents gather near the scene of a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana hugging and consoling one another after a father shot dead eight children in a domestic rampage.

stories.title: Japan issues tsunami warning after powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake
stories.summary: Authorities have urged residents of the affected areas to seek higher ground, heed safety instructions and exercise caution.

stories.title: Turkey rallies global middle powers in Antalya amid Hormuz crisis
stories.summary: At the Antalya summit, Erdoğan brought together leaders of 150 countries to tackle crises, promote alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz, and showcase the growing diplomatic clout of middle powers.

stories.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
stories.summary: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.

stories.title: Rescuers evacuate hundreds after snowstorm hits Russia’s Far East
stories.summary: Dozens of vehicles were rescued from deep snowdrifts in Zabaikalsky Krai, where over 250 people were taken to shelters after a rare April snowstorm.

stories.title: Nigerian wins global prize for trying to save bats in a country that shuns them
stories.summary: Many Nigerians associate bats with witchcraft but that did not deter ecologist Iroro Tanshi.

stories.title: Several killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages – video report
stories.summary: Ukrainian investigators are examining whether a terrorist attack in Kyiv was directed by Moscow, after a man shot dead six people on Saturday before he was killed by police. The gunman, 58, was a Ukrainian citizen born in Moscow. He opened fire on passersby before barricading himself in a supermarket and taking hostages. Detectives sealed off the area in the Holosiivskyi district and tried to negotiate with him. He was killed after a 40-minute standoff Investigators examine whether Ukraine terro

stories.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
stories.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

stories.title: Labour’s ‘crabwise’ approach to closer EU ties must address damage of Brexit | Heather Stewart
stories.summary: The damage to the economy dwarfs the upsides from the various non-EU trade deals the UK has struck since 2016 Rachel Reeves joined EU finance ministers for dinner in Washington last week, on the sidelines of the International Monetary Fund spring meetings – the first time a chancellor had done so since Brexit. It was the latest symbolic step in Labour’s marked shift towards prioritising closer EU relations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian astronaut’s bon mots help heal wounds from French language row
stories.summary: Jeremy Hansen praised for speaking French in space after Air Canada chief’s linguistic snub exposed tensions and drew rebuke from PM Few people foresaw humanity’s quest for the moon as accurately as the 19th-century French author Jules Verne, whose two works –From the Earth to the Moon and Around the Moon – anticipated many of the features of modern lunar exploration. But Verne’s language had never been spoken in deep space until the Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen uttered four words during Nas

stories.title: UK seeks EU deals on steel and EVs in push for closer economic ties
stories.summary: Agreements would aim to shield British industry from new steel tariffs and stricter rules on electric vehicles due in 2027 Downing Street hopes to secure deals on steel and electric cars with the EU as it seeks to upgrade the post-Brexit economic relationship. Amid economic uncertainty caused by the conflict in the Middle East and strains in relations with the US, Keir Starmer is seeking closer economic ties with the EU. Continue reading...

stories.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
stories.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

stories.title: French billionaire defiant as authors quit top publisher
stories.summary: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
stories.summary: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

stories.title: Iran says deal still 'far off' as Hormuz Strait remains shut
stories.summary: Amid stalled US-Iran negotiations, Iranian officials warning the crucial shipping lane will remain shut until the US lifts its blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: North Korea conducts multiple ballistic missile tests
stories.summary: North Korea fired several short-range ballistic missiles into the East Sea on Sunday, the latest in a series of recent weapons tests that analysts say signal Pyongyang's rejection of South Korea's attempts to ease tensions.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track research on psychedelic drugs
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order Saturday to ease research restrictions on psychedelic drugs like psilocybin and LSD, aiming to accelerate studies into their potential for treating mental health conditions.

stories.title: China hosts humanoid robots in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: China has tested humanoid robots in a real race setting in Beijing. The event highlights advances in endurance, balance and AI behaviour. Engineers see it as a step towards wider use.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff reignites as IRGC defies Iran’s diplomats
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz has been blocked again by Iran, as the IRGC’s hardline stance appears to prevail in Tehran. US Vice President JD Vance will lead the US delegation for talks in Pakistan on Monday, after President Trump said he would not attend.

stories.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
stories.summary: Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies. The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia. Continu

stories.title: More than half of Britons support rejoining EU 10 years on from Brexit vote
stories.summary: Experts say Labour’s ‘halfway house’ approach risks losing support from progressives and ‘red wall’ voters Support for rejoining the EU rather than simply rejoining the single market is growing among British voters, with more than 80% of Labour, Liberal Democrat and Green party supporters favouring this option, according to research mapping voter attitudes 10 years after the Brexit referendum. Labour’s “muted” approach to the issue means it risks losing support among progressive voters and in “r

stories.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
stories.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.
